title: AMC Entertainment Q1 2024 Earnings Preview
text: AMC Entertainment, the largest movie theater chain in the United States, released a preliminary preview of its first-quarter 2024 financial results, demonstrating performance that surpassed Wall Street expectations. The company reported Q1 revenue of $951.4 million, compared to $954.4 million in the same quarter of 2023. The company showed significant improvement in losses, posting a diluted loss per share of 62 cents versus 79 cents that analysts had predicted. This represents substantial improvement from the prior-year loss of $1.71 per share. Wall Street had anticipated revenue of approximately $861.1 million, making AMC's actual performance notably stronger than consensus estimates. CEO Adam Aron stated: We are ebullient about the upcoming film slate, and we expect to see an increasingly strong box office as the year progresses. The company raised $41.8 million from Class A share sales as of Thursday, part of a $250 million initiative begun in March. The company carries approximately $4.5 billion in long-term debt, with over $2.8 billion due in 2026.
